WHERE'S THE REAL ACTORS? WRITERS? CREATIVES? PRODUCERS? I don't see very many these days... Here is what it all boils down to: *These hacks all got EXACTLY TWO CHOICES, and they are; 1. Pull your thumbs out of your asses, or 2. Get out of the f*ckin' way!*

replies.author: @Cycstorm
replies.text: @jamesmorris4258  Why is Jeremy, who is big enough to be considered an eceleb and a public figure allowed to use his platform to lecture the public? You don't seem to have any problem with that.

replies.author: @jamesmorris4258
replies.text: @ he’s a commentator… you do know what a commentator is, right? That’s literally his job, and people choose to subscribe to hear his views. And no, a couple hundred thousand followers does not make you a celebrity.

replies.author: @Cycstorm
replies.text: @jamesmorris4258  Jeremy is an eceleb. He goes to conventions and does panels and fan meet ups where people pay money to see him and hear him speak? Yeah, that's a celebrity. And your justification is that he only has a few hundred thousand subs so its okay? If he grew larger to Nerdrotic or Critical Drinker size would he stop lecturing the public? And all this is okay because he is 'just a commentator' because he doesn't have the same amount of talent as someone like Pedro Pascal so he gets to lecture people. See, what you are engaging in is called 'Cope' which is a form of internal justification for beliefs that make no sense at all. Cope Cope cope.

author: @krakenmckraken9128
text: I voted Trump. I am not a confederate. I am not a nazi. Much of my family fought against those forces. However, what I find funny is how Pedro wants to use those groups against the majority of the US population which voted Trump. Pedro said it in this clip. He is a second generation immigrant from Chile. He has no stake in either side of the American Civil War. Aside from benefitting from our country's success once he immigrated. Chile as far as I understand it took no military action during WW2. Regardless of their input, the US won that war. But Pedro thinks labeling Trump supporters (majority of the nation) as the groups that their ancestry had an active part in defeating is the right move? Pedro really isn't helping the case for migrants. Our people have been the best for two hundred years. Our kin put the work in while you only benefitted after the fact. We don't need any more Payyy-dros diluting the population.

replies.author: @Loco0089
replies.text:
First off, slight more than half voted for Trump, and no I would never vote for a racist, moronic felon like Trump. Second, since you voted for an orange felon, do you support the following things that he is doing or has done:
- letting a South African Nazi (Musk) dismantle our government programs an facilities, like USAID and FEMA
- letting said same Nazi try to rig the election in my home state for the Wisconsin State Supreme Court race by bribing voters
- pardoning the Jan 6ers
- gutting the Department of Education
- gutting Medicaid via the "Big Beautiful Bill"
- threaten to annex Canada and Greenland (and considered to use military force on the latter)
- alienating the US from our allies
- mass deporting migrants to an El Salvadoran prison without due process (see Kilmar Albrego Garcia)
- disobeying a direct ruling from the Supreme Court and failing to bring back Kilmar Albrego Garcia
- suggested to run for a third term
- wants to get rid of habeas corpus (a form of due process)
- destroying our economy with recklessly high blanket tariffs
- running meme coin scams while being president (this alone is a very impeachable offense)
- banning transgender people from the military
- 34 felony counts
- wants to get rid of the separation of powers (this is one of the examples of Trump wanting to be a dictator)
- accepting a bribe from Qatar without approval from Congress in the form a $400 million plane for him to keep after he leaves office
